rule: Table columns must be defined before rows are added, and every row must contain exactly the same number of elements as the defined columns.
unit_test: [Fact] public void AddRow_ShouldValidateColumnsAndMatchCount() { var table = new ConsoleTable(); var exceptionNoColumns = Assert.Throws<Exception>(() => table.AddRow("Test")); Assert.Equal("Please set the columns first", exceptionNoColumns.Message); table.AddColumn(new[] { "Column1", "Column2" }); var exceptionMismatchedCount = Assert.Throws<Exception>(() => table.AddRow("OnlyOneValue")); Assert.Contains("does not match the values", exceptionMismatchedCount.Message); table.AddRow("Value1", "Value2"); Assert.Single(table.Rows); }

rule: Table cells exceeding the defined MaxWidth are wrapped or split based on the WordBreakDelimiter, with a preference for breaking at the last occurrence before reaching the width limit.
unit_test: [Fact] public void ShouldWrapAtWordBreakDelimiter() { var table = new ConsoleTable("Column1"); table.MaxWidth = 10; table.SetWordBreakDelimiter(' '); table.AddRow("Hello Worldly"); var result = table.ToString(); Assert.Contains("| Hello   |", result); Assert.Contains("| Worldly |", result); Assert.Contains("Count: 2", result); }

rule: Numeric data types (int, double, decimal, etc.) can be configured to use right-alignment for better readability.
unit_test: [Fact] public void RightAlignmentShouldApplyToDecimalAndDoubleTypes() { var table = new ConsoleTable("Item", "Value"); table.AddRow("Widget", 12.5m); table.AddRow("Gadget", 1.2); table.Configure(o => o.NumberAlignment = Alignment.Right); var output = table.ToString(); Assert.Contains("| Widget | 12.5 |", output); Assert.Contains("| Gadget |  1.2 |", output); }

rule: The table output can optionally display a total row count.
unit_test: [Fact] public void ShouldRespectEnableCountOption() { var tableWithCount = new ConsoleTable("Col1").AddRow("Val1").Configure(o => o.EnableCount = true).ToString(); var tableWithoutCount = new ConsoleTable("Col1").AddRow("Val1").Configure(o => o.EnableCount = false).ToString(); Assert.Contains("Count: 1", tableWithCount); Assert.DoesNotContain("Count:", tableWithoutCount); }

rule: When importing data from a DataTable, binary data (byte arrays) is automatically converted to its Base64 string representation.
unit_test:
[Fact] public void FromDataTable_ShouldConvertByteArrayToBase64() { var data = new DataTable(); data.Columns.Add("Binary", typeof(byte[])); byte[] bytes = { 1, 2, 3 }; data.Rows.Add(bytes); var table = ConsoleTable.From(data); var result = table.ToMarkDownString(); var expectedBase64 = Convert.ToBase64String(bytes); Assert.Equal($"| Binary |
|--------|
| {expectedBase64}   |

", result); }

rule: The library supports populating console-ready tables through manual entry, Enumerable collections, or flat structured POCO objects.
unit_test: [Fact] public void ShouldPopulateTableThroughManualEntryAndPocoCollections() { var manualTable = new ConsoleTable("ID", "Name"); manualTable.AddRow(1, "Manual"); Assert.Equal(2, manualTable.Columns.Count); Assert.Single(manualTable.Rows); var data = new List<User> { new User { Name = "TestUser", Age = 25 } }; var autoTable = ConsoleTable.From(data); Assert.Equal(2, autoTable.Columns.Count); Assert.Contains("Name", autoTable.Columns); Assert.Contains("Age", autoTable.Columns); Assert.Single(autoTable.Rows); }

rule: Tables provide multiple methods for data population, including instantiation from dictionary objects and a fluent interface for adding rows sequentially.
unit_test: [Fact] public void TablePopulation_ShouldSupportDictionaryAndFluentInterface() { var dictionaryData = new Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, object>> { { "Key1", new Dictionary<string, object> { { "Val1", "A" }, { "Val2", "B" } } } }; var tableFromDict = ConsoleTable.FromDictionary(dictionaryData); Assert.NotNull(tableFromDict); Assert.Equal(3, tableFromDict.Columns.Count); Assert.Single(tableFromDict.Rows); var tableFluent = new ConsoleTable("Col1", "Col2").AddRow("R1C1", "R1C2").AddRow("R2C1", "R2C2"); Assert.NotNull(tableFluent); Assert.Equal(2, tableFluent.Columns.Count); Assert.Equal(2, tableFluent.Rows.Count); }

rule: Table display and formatting options are configurable, including column data alignment and the visibility of the total row count.
unit_test: [Fact] public void ShouldApplyConfigurationForAlignmentAndCountVisibility() { var data = new[] { new { Name = "Test", Value = 123 } }; var table = ConsoleTable.From(data).Configure(o => { o.NumberAlignment = Alignment.Right; o.EnableCount = false; }).ToString(); Assert.Contains("|  123 |", table); Assert.DoesNotContain("Count:", table); }

rule: Tables must support precise column alignment and layout, including visual width calculations for multi-byte Unicode characters and configurable alignment for numeric data types.
The table row count must accurately reflect the number of data rows added.
unit_test: [Fact] public void RowCountShouldBeAccurate() { var table = new ConsoleTable("Column1", "Column2").AddRow("Value 1", "Value 2").AddRow("Value 3", "Value 4").AddRow("Value 5", "Value 6"); Assert.Equal(3, table.Rows.Count); string output = table.ToString(); Assert.Contains("Count: 3", output); }

rule: Tables must be constructible from various standard .NET data containers, including List<T>, Dictionary, DataTable, and Object arrays.
unit_test: [Fact] public void ShouldBeConstructibleFromVariousContainers() { var users = new List<User> { new User { Name = "Test User", Age = 25 } }; var tableFromList = ConsoleTable.From(users); Assert.NotNull(tableFromList); Assert.Single(tableFromList.Rows); var dataTable = new System.Data.DataTable(); dataTable.Columns.Add("Name"); dataTable.Columns.Add("Age"); dataTable.Rows.Add("Test User", 25); var tableFromDataTable = ConsoleTable.From(dataTable); Assert.NotNull(tableFromDataTable); Assert.Single(tableFromDataTable.Rows); var objectArray = new object[][] { new object[] { "Name", "Age" }, new object[] { "Test User", 25 } }; var tableFromArray = ConsoleTable.From(objectArray); Assert.NotNull(tableFromArray); Assert.Single(tableFromArray.Rows); var dictionaryData = new Dictionary<string, IEnumerable<object>> { { "Test User", new List<object> { 25 } } }; var tableFromDict = ConsoleTable.FromDictionary(dictionaryData); Assert.NotNull(tableFromDict); Assert.Single(tableFromDict.Rows); }

rule: The system must support multiple output formats and configurable output targets, such as the system console or any TextWriter.
unit_test: [Fact] public void OutputShouldSupportSpecifiedFormatAndCustomWriter() { var table = new ConsoleTables.ConsoleTable("Header"); table.AddRow("Value"); var writer = new System.IO.StringWriter(); table.Configure(options => options.OutputTo = writer); table.Write(ConsoleTables.Format.MarkDown); string output = writer.ToString(); Assert.Contains("| Header |", output); Assert.Contains("| Value  |", output); }
